name: How long do spoken search queries usually take to process and answer?

mainEntity.name: How long do spoken search queries usually take to process and answer?

acceptedAnswer.text:
A spoken search query typically takes 1–3 seconds to recognize your voice and another 1–3 seconds to fetch and read a result, depending on your connection.
On fast Wi‑Fi, responses can feel almost instantaneous.

**Key factors:**
- Internet speed and network stability
- Device processing power and assistant used
- Length of your query and complexity of the answer
- Whether the assistant is loading apps, maps, or media

If responses feel slow, test your network, close unused apps, and shorten your query to the essential question.
For time-sensitive tasks like directions around Los Angeles traffic, start with a simple spoken query, then refine once navigation is running.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most spoken search queries answer in 2–6 seconds end-to-end, depending mainly on your internet speed and device. Short, focused questions on solid Wi‑Fi feel nearly instant, while complex or map-heavy requests can take a bit longer.
